---
title: "Glossary ‚Äî widget vocabulary"
---

> **What this page is:** every load-bearing term in the cositos docs, defined once in plain
> language. Other pages link here on first use. If a page uses a word you don't recognise,
> it's probably here.

## The essentials

**Widget** ‚Äî an interactive UI element (a slider, button, plot‚Ä¶) that lives in a notebook
and can exchange state between the notebook's language and the browser.

**Kernel** ‚Äî the notebook's language engine: the process running your Python, Julia, R, or
Clojure code. The kernel is the *backend* side of a widget.

**Frontend** ‚Äî the JavaScript running in the browser (JupyterLab, Notebook, or a static
page) that draws the widget and reacts to clicks. cositos reuses
[anywidget](https://anywidget.dev)'s frontend, so you write no JavaScript beyond your
widget's own ESM.

**comm** ‚Äî short for *communication channel*. The bidirectional message pipe Jupyter opens
between the kernel and the frontend. When you click a button, a `comm` message carries the
new state kernel‚Üîbrowser. The three message types are `comm_open`, `comm_msg`, and
`comm_close`.

**ESM** ‚Äî an *ECMAScript module*: a JavaScript file with an `export default`. A widget's ESM
is the small JS module that draws it ‚Äî `export default { render({ model, el }) { ‚Ä¶ } }`. In
cositos you paste it verbatim (you don't need to write JS to *run* a widget), or author your
own in the [authoring tutorial](authoring-widgets.qmd).

**anywidget** ‚Äî an open standard for building widgets from a single ESM module. cositos
targets anywidget's published frontend (`AnyModel`/`AnyView`), which is why a cositos widget
renders anywhere anywidget is installed.

## State & model terms

**Model** ‚Äî the shared state object between kernel and browser. In the ESM you call
`model.get(key)` / `model.set(key, value)` / `model.save_changes()` to read and push state.

**AnyModel / AnyView** ‚Äî anywidget's generic frontend model and view. Every plain cositos
widget declares itself as `AnyModel`; the browser loads that class to render it.

**trait** ‚Äî a declared, typed field on a widget model (from ipywidgets' *traitlets*).
"A widget-reference trait" is a field whose value is *another* widget; plain `AnyModel`
widgets don't declare these, which is why references between them stay literal strings.

**buffer** ‚Äî a chunk of binary data (e.g. a `float32` array) carried *out-of-band* alongside
the JSON state, per the widget protocol v2. `remove_buffers` splits them out of the state
tree; `put_buffers` merges them back.

**\_esm** ‚Äî the state key holding a widget's ESM source string.

## Lifecycle & architecture terms

**reducer** ‚Äî a pure function `reduce(phase, event, state, capabilities) ‚Üí (new_phase,
effects)` that encodes the widget's open‚Üísend‚Üíclose lifecycle as a deterministic state
machine (the same idea as Redux/Elm). It performs no I/O; a thin *shell* runs its effects.

**Transport** ‚Äî the only kernel-facing interface the core depends on: a thin adapter over a
kernel's comm API (`send`, `on_message`). Each host supplies one.

**Channel** ‚Äî the frontend-side counterpart to Transport: how the browser side sends/receives
(e.g. `LocalChannel` for kernel-free demos, `PlutoChannel` for Pluto, `ClayChannel` for Clay).

**mimebundle** ‚Äî the payload Jupyter asks an object for when displaying it
(`_repr_mimebundle_`). Rendering a cositos widget returns its view mimebundle.

**Document** ‚Äî a plain-JSON snapshot of one or more widgets' state (the *Widget State JSON
schema v2*), produced by `dump_document` and rebuilt by `load_document`.

**MVU** ‚Äî *Model‚ÄìView‚ÄìUpdate*: an app pattern where all state lives in one model, an
`update` function is the only thing that changes it, and views are pure projections of it.
The recommended way to keep a multi-widget cositos app consistent
(see [state discipline](state-discipline.qmd)).

## Versions & status

**protocol version (`2.1.0`)** ‚Äî the widget *messaging* protocol cositos speaks on the wire.

**state-format version (`2.0`)** ‚Äî the *serialization* schema version for a saved `Document`.
Distinct from the protocol version ‚Äî a common point of confusion.

**Tier 1** ‚Äî a kernel with a fully live comm round-trip, verified in CI (Python/ipykernel,
Julia/IJulia, Clojure/Clay). See [`probe/README.md`](https://github.com/sashakile/cositos/blob/main/probe/README.md)
for the full tier definitions.

**golden fixtures** ‚Äî the canonical `fixtures/*.json` files every language port must
reproduce byte-for-byte; the shared certification target.
